channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police and military complete security preparations for BARMM's first parliamentary elections on September 14
item.description: Police and military forces have completed their security preparations for the first-ever parliamentary elections in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), which are scheduled on ...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules Ransom Money Not Required as Evidence in Kidnapping Cases
item.description: The Supreme Court (SC) ruled that actual ransom money does not need to be presented in court to secure a conviction for kidnapping for ransom. In a decision penned by Associate Justice Ricardo Rosario...

item.title: KSE-100 edges higher amid global energy market volatility
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange's (PSX) benchmark index, the KSE-100, recorded marginal gains on Tuesday as investor sentiment remained cautious due to uncertainties in global energy markets. The index op...

item.title: Reader Letters and Humorous Musings from Column 8
item.description: The article features a column titled 'Column 8' from The Age, showcasing reader-submitted letters that blend humor with cultural references. Contributors share anecdotes about travel experiences, such...

item.title: Small Businesses in Calgary Concerned Over Potential Milk Shortages Following Plant Incident
item.description: The article discusses growing concerns among small businesses in Calgary regarding potential milk shortages linked to an electrical issue at a Saputo plant. The incident, which occurred on July 20, 20...

item.title: ADNOC's Strategic Expansion Across Africa's Energy Markets
item.description: The Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) is intensifying its efforts to establish a significant presence across multiple African countries through strategic acquisitions and partnerships. This move ...

item.title: Former Warri Refinery MD charged with money laundering, gets N500m bail
item.description: A former Managing Director of the Warri Refining and Petrochemical Company Limited, Jimoh Yisawu, was arraigned before the Federal High Court in Abuja on Monday over alleged money laundering charges. ...

item.title: Congolese salon owner faces xenophobic confrontation over business ownership
item.description: A Congolese entrepreneur in Melmoth, South Africa, destroyed her own salon equipment after being confronted by a group claiming foreign nationals cannot own salons. The woman, who is legally residing ...

item.title: ICPC probes Chief of Staff over alleged fake agency scandal
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) has summoned Chief of Staff to President Bola Tinubu, Femi Gbajabiamila, to investigate allegations linked to a fraudulen...

item.title: Zambales Fisherfolk Advocate Peaceful Resolution of West Philippine Sea Disputes
item.description: Two fisherfolk associations in Zambales have expressed support for a peaceful, diplomatic resolution of tensions in the West Philippine Sea, backing a national fishers’ group’s call to demilitarize th...

item.title: Photo Animation Highlights Key Moments of World Cup 2026
item.description: The article describes a dynamic photo animation created by Picture editor Jonny Weeks, which provides a rapid visual and auditory overview of the World Cup 2026 tournament. The video compiles key mome...

item.title: Trump's Endorsement of Lindsey Graham's Sister Faces Resistance in South Carolina Senate Race
item.description: A growing political battle is unfolding in South Carolina after President Donald Trump endorsed Darline Graham Nordone, the sister of the late Senator Lindsey Graham, as the Republican candidate for t...

item.title: Ottawa City Hall gifts reveal diplomatic and bureaucratic traditions
item.description: The article explores the ceremonial practice of gifting at Ottawa City Hall, where visiting dignitaries and departing public servants receive symbolic presents. These gifts, often crafted by local art...

item.title: Farmers Protest Against Proposed India-US Trade Deal
item.description: Hundreds of farmers from Punjab were blocked at the Punjab-Haryana border on Tuesday while attempting to enter Delhi for a planned farmers' congregation to protest against the proposed India-US trade ...

item.title: George Wajackoyah Turned Away from Church Youth Event by Bishop Muheria
item.description: Roots Party leader George Wajackoyah was turned away from an AMECEA Youth Mass held at Queen of Apostles Parish in Ruaraka, Nairobi, by Nyeri Archbishop Anthony Muheria. The bishop cited the Catholic ...

item.title: Transnet invests $7.2bn to rebuild infrastructure and reduce debt
item.description: Transnet Group CEO Michelle Phillips has detailed the company's five-year $7.2 billion capital plan aimed at rehabilitating core infrastructure, reducing debt costs, and enhancing freight efficiency t...

item.title: podcast host rehan tariq granted post-arrest bail in blasphemy case
item.description: a sessions court in lahore granted post-arrest bail to podcast host rehan tariq in a blasphemy case registered under the prevention of electronic crimes act (peca). the national cyber crime investigat...

item.title: Section 89 Committee Reviews ActionSA's Phala Phala Evidence in Impeachment Inquiry
item.description: The parliamentary Section 89 committee investigating President Cyril Ramaphosa's potential impeachment will review fresh evidence submitted by ActionSA, focusing on disciplinary findings against two p...

item.title: Mass Protests in Delhi Signal Growing Dissent Against Modi Government
item.description: The international media highlighted the massive protests in Delhi against the BJP government, framing them as one of the largest expressions of dissent in recent years. The Cockroach Janta Party's mar...

item.title: Meta's Smartglasses and the Risks of Covert Child Filming in the Age of AI
item.description: The article discusses the potential risks of Meta's new smartglasses, which can covertly film children, raising concerns about privacy and the misuse of AI technology. It references a personal anecdot...
